feedback.text: Good evening. Companies, Visa/Mastercard, should not influence what we consume for media, especially when they put financial pressure on traders (rejection of payment transactions). In my view, this is a cultural censorship/repression and practice is similar to a cartel/monopoly. Collective SHOUT, an Australian activist group, has influenced Visa and Mastercard to force Steam and itch.io to remove certain games/books from their market despite being legal. The reason for this is the themes in the games/books that do not match their opinion. In a dystopian future, social issues such as history or family values are brought under censorship, which would be possible with this action. As long as it is legal, payment liquidators (here: Visa and Mastercard) do not prevent our purchases.
